title: KEYCORP DECLARES QUARTERLY CASH DIVIDEND ON COMMON SHARES AND PREFERRED STOCKS AND ANNOUNCES NEW SHARE REPURCHASE PROGRAM
author: Prnewswire
description: KeyCorp announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.205 per share on common stock payable June 15, 2026, along with dividends on multiple preferred stock series. The company also authorized a new $3.0 billion share repurchase program, replacing its previous $1.0 billion authorization with approximately $280 million remaining.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: KeyCorp's announcement of consistent dividend payments and a substantial $3.0 billion share repurchase program demonstrates strong capital management and commitment to shareholder returns. The tripling of the repurchase authorization from $1.0 billion to $3.0 billion signals management confidence in the company's financial position and future prospects.

title: Stock Market Today, Jan. 22: Huntington Bancshares Falls After Missing Revenue and EPS Estimates
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Huntington Bancshares stock fell 6.02% on January 22, 2026, after missing Q4 2025 revenue and EPS estimates due to higher expenses and credit provisions. Despite 12% revenue growth, the bank's criticized asset ratio rose and management guided for 10% expense increases in 2026. However, management expects strong growth in net interest income, loans, deposits, and fee revenue, with full synergies from acquisitions expected by mid-2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 6.02% due to missing revenue and EPS estimates, rising criticized asset ratio (3.79% to 4.23%), and 10% expected expense increase in 2026. However, positive guidance on revenue growth and acquisition synergies partially offset the negative reaction.

title: KEYCORP DECLARES QUARTERLY CASH DIVIDEND ON COMMON SHARES AND PREFERRED STOCKS; SETS ANNUAL MEETING DATE
author: Prnewswire
description: KeyCorp announced quarterly cash dividends for Q1 2026, including $0.205 per share on common stock and various dividend amounts on preferred stocks, with payment dates in March 2026. The company also set its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders for May 14, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: KeyCorp's declaration of consistent quarterly dividends across multiple share classes demonstrates financial stability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. The maintenance of dividend payments indicates confidence in the company's financial position and cash flow generation capabilities.

title: 3 High-Yield Stocks From Canada to Buy With $1,000 and Hold Forever
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: The article recommends three Canadian stocks for U.S. investors seeking diversification and high dividend yields: Bank of Nova Scotia (4.2% yield) as a low-risk turnaround play, Brookfield Renewable Partners (5.3% yield) as a clean energy leader with consistent distribution growth, and Enbridge (5.8% yield) as a stable midstream operator with 30 years of dividend increases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positioned as a low-risk turnaround with strong regulatory foundation, conservative operations, and attractive 4.2% yield supported by 190+ years of dividend payments. Management's strategic refocus on Mexico, U.S., and Canada markets shows progress.

Highlighted as a clean energy leader with significant long-term growth opportunity across hydroelectric, solar, wind, and nuclear power. Offers 5.3% yield with consistent annual distribution increases of 5-9% planned for the foreseeable future.

title: Billionaire David Tepper Dumped Appaloosa's Stake in Oracle and Is Piling Into a Sector That Wall Street Thinks Will Outperform
author: Bram Berkowitz
description: Billionaire investor David Tepper sold his entire Oracle stake in Q3 and shifted investments into the financial sector, which Wall Street analysts expect to outperform despite recent underperformance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong AI potential but concerns about infrastructure costs and margin compression led Tepper to exit his position
